description_long: This is a classic, single-propeller, fixed-wing airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a bright red exterior. The aircraft features a low-wing configuration with straight wings and rounded wingtips, and a conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The nose houses a visible radial piston engine with a two-blade propeller, and the cockpit is positioned just behind the engine, likely accommodating a single pilot. The landing gear is of the taildragger type, with two main wheels under the wings and a small tail wheel at the rear. The surface appears smooth and metallic, with minimal visible markings or windows. The overall design suggests a vintage or sport aircraft, suitable for display or light recreational use in an urban context.
description: A bright red, vintage-style single-propeller airplane with a low-wing, taildragger configuration and a streamlined, smooth metallic body.
description_view_0: Side view showing the streamlined fuselage, low-mounted straight wing, and exposed radial engine with a two-blade propeller.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the full wingspan, rounded wingtips, and the alignment of the tail and main landing gear.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the taildragger landing gear and the smooth, uninterrupted fuselage.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the circular radial engine, two-blade propeller, and the symmetrical wing and tail arrangement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with rounded wingtips and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted radial piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed wheels and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit located just behind the engine.
